title: The Preliminary List
caption: Detective Park arrives with such immediate seriousness that Chloe barely has time to finish her explanation before the mystery belongs to both of them. On the quiet residential sidewalk, he studies the carefully placed flower as though it were a witness that has not yet decided to speak. When Chloe asks whether a flower can really leave evidence, he answers, with absolute conviction, that even kindness leaves a trace. Then, to her astonishment, he produces a preliminary suspect list so long it spills across the pavement like a parade banner. As he solemnly includes gardeners, former gardeners, admirers of gardeners, and anyone with unresolved feelings about begonias, Chloe cannot help laughing. Still, beneath the joke, she feels the strange excitement of a real investigation taking shape. In Willowmere, even a single flower can open a very large case.
location: A quiet residential sidewalk in Willowmere, outside a row of front porches marked by mysterious flowers
story_notes: Detective Park immediately joins Chloe's flower mystery investigation and reveals an absurdly long suspect list.
